message:
test(readme): integration test for DataFrame Output Formats (fd90.189 / br-f6vzb)

Adds readme_dataframe_output_formats_compiles_and_runs covering 11 of
the 12 inline DataFrame output methods documented in README lines
530-543. Display trait is implicitly tested elsewhere.

Coverage:
- to_csv (sep + include_index)
- to_json (records + columns orients)
- to_string_table / to_string_truncated (max_rows truncation)
- to_html / to_latex / to_markdown
- to_dict (dict/list/records/split orients)
- to_series_dict (BTreeMap<String, Series>)
- to_records (Vec<Vec<Scalar>>; rows include the index label as first
  element, so row length is column_count + 1 — caught during initial
  test write, asserted explicitly to lock in this behavior)
- to_numpy_2d (numeric coercion to Vec<Vec<f64>>)

Found via self-review on the last 3 commits — these 11 methods all exist
on DataFrame in fp-frame but had drifted out of integration coverage.

Verified: 28 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -386,6 +386,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-f51i","title":"[HIGH] REVIEW: IO live oracle dtype/options parity drifts","description":"Found while building frankenpandas-czmt IO conformance matrix.

1. csv_read_frame with csv_decimal=",", input price\n"1,50"\n"3,75"\n. Expected from live pandas: ["1,50", "3,75"] strings. Actual FrankenPandas: [1.5, 3.75] floats.

2. csv_read_frame with csv_true_values=["1"], csv_false_values=["0"], input flag\n1\n0\n. Expected from live pandas: [1, 0] ints. Actual FrankenPandas: [true, false] bools.

3. csv_read_frame missing-heavy numeric column, input a,b,c\n,NA,NaN\n1,,x\n. Expected from live pandas: column a [null, 1.0]. Actual FrankenPandas: [null, 1].

4. dataframe_to_json_records with duplicate index and column a=[1, null]. Expected live pandas JSON has 1.0 because null promotes numeric column to float. Actual FrankenPandas JSON preserves 1.

5. csv_read_frame with csv_parse_dates=["ts"], input ts,value\n2024-01-15 10:30:00,1\n2024-01-15T10:30:00Z,2\n. Expected from live pandas: second ts remains "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z" under inferred-format parsing. Actual FrankenPandas: "2024-01-15 10:30:00+00:00".

Impact: IO read/serialize paths are not matching pandas dtype coercion, parse_dates, and option precedence for these edge cases.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T06:23:07.337232398Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T08:01:31.840746962Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T08:01:31.838869158Z","close_reason":"Fixed IO dtype/options parity drifts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","parity","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-f5kz","title":"pandas: DataFrame.to_markdown(tablefmt='heavy_grid') parity","description":"Python signature: pd.DataFrame.to_markdown(index=..., tablefmt='heavy_grid'). fp-frame currently rejects heavy_grid via the unsupported to_markdown tablefmt branch. Add the heavy-grid box drawing renderer in crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs and lock it down with exact-output tests for index=False and index=True without regressing the existing table formats.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:51:44.283142770Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:25:23.079655344Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T19:25:23.077832914Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-f64w","title":"fp-columnar ValidityMask.first_valid/last_valid parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:24:54.466171816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:28:28.374952799Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:28:28.373176054Z","close_reason":"Implemented ValidityMask::first_valid/last_valid in commit 29b76f1, 1 test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-f6vzb","title":"fd90.189: Integration test for README DataFrame Output Formats table","description":"fd90.189: Add integration test for README "DataFrame Output Formats" table (lines 530-543).

The README documents 12 inline output methods on DataFrame, none of which have integration coverage in tests/readme_quick_example.rs.

Scope (one new test):
- DataFrame.to_csv(sep, include_index)
- DataFrame.to_json(orient)
- DataFrame.to_string_table(include_index)
- DataFrame.to_string_truncated(idx, rows, cols)
- DataFrame.to_html(include_index)
- DataFrame.to_latex(include_index)
- DataFrame.to_markdown(include_index, tablefmt)
- DataFrame.to_dict(orient)
- DataFrame.to_series_dict()
- DataFrame.to_records()
- DataFrame.to_numpy_2d()

Each invocation asserted to return a non-empty result; correctness handled by unit tests.

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (single new test, ~40 lines).
Time estimate: 30-40min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:02:23.879753477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:04:37.305057492Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T19:04:37.302712049Z","close_reason":"Added readme_dataframe_output_formats_compiles_and_runs covering 11 inline output methods: to_csv, to_json (records + columns orients), to_string_table, to_string_truncated (with max_rows truncation), to_html, to_latex, to_markdown, to_dict (4 orients: dict/list/records/split), to_series_dict, to_records (caught implicit index-prepending behavior — row length is column_count + 1, not column_count), to_numpy_2d. All 28 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-f7sjm","title":"fd90.101: README IoError row shows MissingIndexColumn as unit variant but code has MissingIndexColumn(String)","description":"fd90.74-100 README audit. Line 804 IoError row lists 'MissingIndexColumn' as if it were a unit variant, alongside 'Csv(...)', 'Json(...)', etc. that correctly show parens for tuple variants. But crates/fp-io/src/lib.rs:113 declares it as MissingIndexColumn(String). The other unit variant on this line is MissingHeaders (no inner data) which is correct. Fix: append (...) to MissingIndexColumn so the README convention matches the code. Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:58:03.118168070Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:59:09.022137820Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:59:09.019449444Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
